summary: Comprehensive testing of Tutor Metodológico (FastAPI + React + MongoDB + Claude Sonnet 4.5 via Emergent LLM Key). Backend: 10/10 pytest tests passed covering session CRUD, multi-turn chat, 3-section format, validation errors. Frontend: end-to-end flow works — empty state, 4 suggestions, session creation via suggestion click, 3-section rendering (archivo/paso/cierre), Enter to send, sidebar list & delete. No emojis and no final answer (e.g., '5') leaked in tutor responses. Tutor strictly follows the format protocol.

integration_issues.flow: Send message via suggestion (first message in new session)
integration_issues.issue: React console warning: 'Encountered two children with the same key' for message UUIDs. Caused by race between optimistic update + fetchMessages effect (triggered by activeSessionId change in newSession) + setMessages with backend payload. Cosmetic; UI still renders correctly. Suggestion: in sendMessage, dedupe by id when merging, e.g., setMessages((m) => { const map = new Map(m.filter(x => x.id !== tempId).map(x => [x.id, x])); map.set(data.user_message.id, data.user_message); map.set(data.assistant_message.id, data.assistant_message); return Array.from(map.values()); }).

action_items: Optional: deduplicate messages by id in sendMessage merge logic to silence React duplicate-key warning (no functional impact).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: `logger` is referenced in send_message exception handler (line ~168) but defined at the bottom of the file (line ~206). Due to module-level execution order, this works at runtime (logger exists by the time requests are handled) but is brittle—move logging.basicConfig and `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` near the top of the file.

server.py: history is fed via system prompt concatenation rather than proper chat history; LlmChat per-request is fine, but consider passing history as messages for cleaner multi-turn semantics. Currently works.
server.py: no length cap on history_block — could grow unbounded across long sessions and increase token costs / latency. Consider truncating to last N turns.
App.js: race between fetchMessages (triggered by activeSessionId effect) and optimistic setMessages can produce duplicate entries — dedupe by id when merging.
App.js: sendMessage from EmptyState calls newSession() which sets activeSessionId; fetchMessages is then called for the new session before the LLM POST returns — minor inefficiency.
seed_data_creation: Tests create and clean up their own sessions (10 sessions created and torn down).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_tutor_api.py and pass in ~8s. Frontend renders 3 sections via data-testid=section-archivo-datos / section-paso-activo / section-accion-cierre. Tutor system prompt enforces no final answer; spot-checked with Pythagoras and quadratic factoring — neither leaked the answer. Real Claude Sonnet 4.5 calls via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY work.
